description:
Goal: simplify the in-house backend until the compiler2-native handoff is truthful and self-sufficient. Signal: the compiler2-native backend contract is expressed directly in compiler2-owned data structures; no compiler2-native consumer needs copied `SpecKey`/`SpecPlan`/`SpecRegistry`, widened `return_tys`, or old-world semantic payloads hung off `fz_ir` just to answer backend questions.

Change:
- audit each compiler2-native backend question and make the answer live directly on `NativeProgram` / `NativeBody` or another compiler2-owned artifact
- delete copied planner-shaped baggage that survived the fork, replacing it with direct compiler2 facts
- remove or replace old-world semantic payloads that were only present to feed the old shared route, especially extern contracts and dispatch/type-test payloads

Why:
- the fork is only a means to gain ownership; the payoff is deleting the copied fiction rather than preserving it under compiler2 names

Checks:
- compiler2 artifact tests prove the backend reads direct compiler2 facts for body identity, value types, callable entries, continuation ownership, extern metadata, and dispatch/type-test inputs
- the remaining compiler2-native backend questions can be answered without inventing planner state
title: Compiler2 native backends: simplify the in-house backend and make the handoff truthful
